question.text: A bull call spread with 10 lots: Buy 83.00 call at 0.10, Sell 83.50 call at 0.04. USDINR at expiry = 83.30. What is total P&L?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Long 83 call payoff: 83.30-83.00-0.10 = +0.20. Short 83.50 call payoff: 83.30 < 83.50 so not exercised, keep premium +0.04. Net per unit = 0.24. Total = 10 x 1000 x 0.24 = Rs. 2,400. **The Trap:** At 83.30, only the lower strike call is exercised. The higher strike call expires worthless. **Cross-Exam Context:** Intermediate payoff (between strikes) tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Between the two strikes, P&L varies linearly. Below both or above both, P&L is fixed.
